REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, TO RECONSIDER DEFENDANTS MOTION TO DISMISS 11/15/2023 IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISMISS (AND JOINDER) WERE FIRST SET FOR HEARING FOR JULY 21, 2023. PER PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST THE COURT CONTINUED THE HEARING SEVERAL TIMES. ONE DAY BEFORE ONE OF THE CONTINUED HEARING DATES, ON SEPTEMBER 14, 2023, PLAINTIFF FILED A REQUEST FOR CONTINUANCE AND A PURPORTED RESPONSE/OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION. THAT RESPONSE WAS UNTIMELY AND WAS SUBSTANTIVELY DEFICIENT AND DEVOID OF ANY EVIDENCE AND AUTHORITY. THE COURT GAVE PLAINTIFF ANOTHER OPPORTUNITY TO FILE AN OPPOSITION ON THE MERITS ADDRESSING ISSUES RAISED IN THE MOTION TO DISMISS BY NOVEMBER 1, 2023. PLAINTIFF DID NOT AVAIL HERSELF OF THIS OPPORTUNITY. HAVING CONSIDERED THE MOVING PAPERS AND THE FILINGS BY THE P SUBMITTED AFTER THE ORIGINAL FILING OF THE MOTION TO DISMISS AND BEFORE THE HEARING ON NOVEMBER 15, 2023, THE COURT GRANTED THE MOTION TO DISMISS (AND JOINDER). PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION FILED ON NOVEMBER 27, 2023 DOES NOT MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP 1008. THE MOTION MENTIONS CCP 473, BUT THE REQUIREMENTS OF THAT CODE SECTION ARE ALSO NOT MET. THE COURT NOTES THAT IT DID NOT RECEIVE ANY PROPOSED ORDERS FOR EXECUTION AFTER NOVEMBER 15, 2023 HEARING. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: L. YOUNG; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

To Reconsider Defendants Motion To Dismiss 11/15/2023
Off Calendar Mar-27-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Linda Catron; 415-999-1933; Plaintiff in pro per appearing in person.
Adam Summerfield; 213-457-9856; Mcguirewoods LLP appearing via Zoom for defendant Bank of America.

Plaintiff informs the court that documents were filed by her before she appeared for the hearing. She also informs the court that she has not read the tentative ruling. The court allows her to review before proceeding. The court informs plaintiff that the papers filed earlier will not be considered by the court. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LINDA CATRON To Reconsider Defendants Motion To Dismiss 11/15/2023

is DENIED. Defendant's motion to dismiss (and joinder) were first set for hearing for July 21, 2023. Per Plaintiff's request the Court continued the hearing several times. One day before one of the continued hearing dates, on September 14, 2023, Plaintiff filed a request for continuance and a purported response/opposition to the motion. That response was untimely and was substantively deficient and devoid of any evidence and authority. The Court gave Plaintiff another opportunity to file an opposition on the merits addressing issues raised in the Motion to Dismiss by November 1, 2023. Plaintiff did not avail herself of this opportunity.
Having considered the moving papers and the filings by the P submitted after the original filing of the Motion to Dismiss and before the hearing on November 15, 2023, the Court granted the Motion to Dismiss (and joinder). Plaintiff's Motion for Reconsideration filed on November 27, 2023 does not meet the requirements of CCP 1008. The motion mentions CCP 473, but the requirements of that code section are also not met. The Court notes that it did not receive any proposed orders for execution after November 15, 2023 hearing.

Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Young; Not Reported. (D501)
